⬥ Scholar's Preface

Two forces govern this creature simultaneously, and neither answers to the other.

The first is persistent. The ADHD brain does not automate what other brains do silently. The regulation of emotion, the filtering of sensation, the ordering of time; these are performed manually, consciously, at considerable cost.

The second is tidal. During perimenopause, oestrogen (which directly governs dopamine, the very neurotransmitter the ADHD brain is already short of) rises and falls without schedule or warning. On some days the tide is high and the work is manageable. On others it recedes entirely, and the manual system must operate on reserves it does not have.

These are the Twin Tides. Two biological systems, each already demanding, fluctuating in compound with one another in ways that even the creature herself cannot always predict or explain.

You will not find a cure in these pages. What you will find is a method of reading the water — of identifying which tide is running, and what the conditions require of you in that hour.

That is sufficient. That is, in fact, everything.

Have Courage

When the creature lashes out. When the words are too sharp and retreat beckons, remember,

She is battling two tides that threaten to drag her under. What is enduring a sharp word, compared to that?

She is your shield-sister. She shuld not have to fight alone.

Codex Simia
The Vedmak's Stores
